info.title: Bonsai Operator API
info.description: API documentation for Bonsai Operator API with JWT authentication

offset.description: Starting index for pagination (default: 0)

limit.description: Maximum number of items to return. Defaults to 100; maximum 1000

orderBy.description: Field(s) to sort by. Use "-" prefix for descending order (e.g., "-createdAt")

filters.description: Dynamic field filters as key-value pairs. Use bracket notation in query string (e.g., filters[projectId]=value, filters[name][op]=like&filters[name][value]=test). Values can be direct values, arrays (for IN), or operation objects

ListParams.description: List query parameters for filtering, sorting, pagination, and search

op.description: Filter operator: eq (equals), ne (not equals), gt (greater than), gte (>=), lt (less than), lte (<=), like (pattern match), in (value in array), nin (not in array), between (range)

value.description: Filter value to compare against. For "in", "nin", and "between" operations, use an array

ListFilterOperation.description: Filter operation with explicit operator and value

version.description: The current version number for optimistic locking

offset.description: Starting index for pagination (default: 0)

limit.description: Maximum number of items to return. Defaults to 100; maximum 1000

orderBy.description: Field(s) to sort by. Use "-" prefix for descending order (e.g., "-createdAt")

filters.description: Dynamic field filters as key-value pairs. Use bracket notation in query string (e.g., filters[projectId]=value, filters[name][op]=like&filters[name][value]=test). Values can be direct values, arrays (for IN), or operation objects

archived.description: When true, returns only archived projects. When omitted or false, returns only active (non-archived) projects.

model.description: Model name (e.g., gpt-4, gpt-3.5-turbo, gpt-5, o1)

defaultMaxTokens.description: Default maximum output tokens for generation (includes reasoning and output tokens for reasoning models)

defaultTemperature.description: Default temperature for generation (0-2). Not used with reasoning models - use reasoningEffort instead.

defaultTopP.description: Default top-p for generation (0-1). Not used with reasoning models - use reasoningEffort instead.

reasoningEffort.description: Reasoning effort for reasoning models (gpt-5, o-series). Controls how many reasoning tokens to generate. low=fast/economical, high=more complete reasoning. Default: medium. gpt-5.1 defaults to none.

reasoningSummary.description: Generate a summary of reasoning performed by the model. Useful for debugging. Only for reasoning models.

defaultMaxTokens.description: Default maximum tokens for generation (includes thinking tokens when extended thinking is enabled)

defaultTemperature.description: Default temperature for generation (0-1). Not compatible with extended thinking.

defaultTopP.description: Default top-p for generation (0-1). Limited to 0.95-1 when thinking is enabled.

thinkingMode.description: Enable extended thinking. Use "adaptive" for Claude Opus 4.6+, "enabled" for earlier models. Allows Claude to reason internally before responding.

thinkingBudgetTokens.description: Maximum tokens for internal reasoning (min: 1024). Only used with thinkingMode="enabled". Higher budgets enable deeper reasoning but increase latency.

model.description: Model name (e.g., gemini-2.5-flash, gemini-2.5-pro, gemini-3-flash, gemini-3-pro)

defaultMaxTokens.description: Default maximum tokens for generation (includes thinking tokens for thinking models)

thinkingLevel.description: Thinking level for Gemini 3 models. Controls reasoning depth: minimal=chat/high-throughput, low=simple tasks, medium=balanced, high=max reasoning depth.

thinkingBudget.description: Thinking budget (tokens) for Gemini 2.5 models. Set to -1 for dynamic thinking (default), 0 to disable, or specific token count (128-32768). Use thinkingLevel for Gemini 3.

includeThoughts.description: Include thought summaries in response. Provides insight into model's reasoning process for debugging. Available for all thinking models.

model.description: Model name in OpenRouter format (e.g., openai/gpt-4o, anthropic/claude-3-5-sonnet, meta-llama/llama-3.3-70b-instruct)

LlmSettings.description: LLM provider-specific settings for this stage

model.description: Model ID to use for speech synthesis (e.g., "eleven_flash_v2_5", "eleven_multilingual_v2")

voiceId.description: Voice UUID to use for speech synthesis

audioFormat.description: Preferred audio output format for synthesized speech

noSpeechMarkers.description: Markers to identify sections of text that should not be spoken

removeExclamationMarks.description: Whether to replace exclamation marks with periods

style.description: Style setting for V2+ models (0.0-1.0), defaults to 0

useSpeakerBoost.description: Enable speaker boost for V2+ models, defaults to true

useGlobalPreview.description: Use global preview endpoint for geographic proximity optimization

inactivityTimeout.description: WebSocket inactivity timeout in seconds, defaults to 180

useSentenceSplitter.description: Whether to use sentence splitter for text processing, defaults to true

model.description: Model ID to use for speech synthesis: "gpt-4o-mini-tts" (promptable), "tts-1" (low latency), or "tts-1-hd" (high quality)

voiceId.description: Voice ID to use (alloy, ash, ballad, coral, echo, fable, nova, onyx, sage, shimmer, verse, marin, cedar)

audioFormat.description: Preferred audio output format for synthesized speech

instructions.description: Voice control instructions for gpt-4o-mini-tts model. Controls accent, tone, emotion, speed, whispering, etc. Only supported by gpt-4o-mini-tts model

noSpeechMarkers.description: Markers to identify sections of text that should not be spoken

removeExclamationMarks.description: Whether to replace exclamation marks with periods

useSentenceSplitter.description: Whether to use sentence splitter for text processing, defaults to true

voiceId.description: Voice ID to use for speech synthesis (e.g., "thalia-en", "andromeda-en"). Combined with model to form full model string (e.g., "aura-2-thalia-en")

audioFormat.description: Preferred audio output format. Defaults to "pcm_16000"

sampleRate.description: Sample rate for audio output in Hz (e.g., 8000, 16000, 24000, 48000). Availability depends on audio format

bitRate.description: Bit rate for audio output (e.g., 32000, 64000, 128000). Applies to certain formats like mp3, opus, aac

container.description: Audio container format. Use "none" for raw audio, "wav" for WAV container, "ogg" for Ogg container

noSpeechMarkers.description: Markers to identify sections of text that should not be spoken

removeExclamationMarks.description: Whether to replace exclamation marks with periods

useSentenceSplitter.description: Whether to use sentence splitter for text processing, defaults to true

model.description: Model ID to use for speech synthesis (e.g., "sonic-3", "sonic-3-latest", "sonic-3-2026-01-12"). Defaults to "sonic-3-latest"

voiceId.description: Voice ID to use for speech synthesis (e.g., "f786b574-daa5-4673-aa0c-cbe3e8534c02" for Katie). See Cartesia voice catalog

language.description: Language code for speech synthesis (e.g., "en", "es", "fr"). Sonic-3 supports 42 languages

audioFormat.description: Preferred audio output format for synthesized speech. Defaults to "pcm_24000"

emotion.description: Emotion tags for expressive speech (e.g., ["positivity:high", "curiosity"]). See Cartesia emotion documentation

maxBufferDelayMs.description: Maximum time in milliseconds to buffer text chunks before sending to TTS (0-5000ms). Defaults to 3000ms. Set to 0 to disable buffering

useSentenceSplitter.description: Whether to use sentence splitter for text processing. Defaults to false (uses streaming with continuations instead)

noSpeechMarkers.description: Markers to identify sections of text that should not be spoken

removeExclamationMarks.description: Whether to replace exclamation marks with periods

model.description: Azure TTS model to use. Currently only "neural" is supported for high-quality neural text-to-speech

voiceId.description: Voice name to use for speech synthesis (e.g., "en-US-AriaNeural", "en-US-GuyNeural")

audioFormat.description: Preferred audio output format for synthesized speech. Defaults to "pcm_24000"

style.description: Speaking style for voices that support it (e.g., "cheerful", "sad", "angry", "friendly")

rate.description: Speaking rate adjustment (e.g., "+10%", "-5%", "1.2"). Range: 0.5 to 2.0 or percentage

useSentenceSplitter.description: Whether to use sentence splitter for text processing. Defaults to true

noSpeechMarkers.description: Markers to identify sections of text that should not be spoken

removeExclamationMarks.description: Whether to replace exclamation marks with periods

voiceId.description: Voice ID to use for speech synthesis (e.g., "Joanna", "Matthew", "Amy"). Defaults to "Joanna"

engine.description: Polly engine to use. "neural" provides higher quality, "long-form" supports longer texts, "generative" provides most natural speech. Defaults to "neural"

languageCode.description: BCP-47 language code (e.g., "en-US", "en-GB", "es-ES"). By default inferred from the selected voice

audioFormat.description: Preferred audio output format. "mp3" for compressed audio, "pcm_8000" or "pcm_16000" for raw PCM. Defaults to "pcm_16000"

useSentenceSplitter.description: Whether to split text into sentences and synthesize each individually. Defaults to false (full text is synthesized when end() is called)

noSpeechMarkers.description: Markers to identify sections of text that should not be spoken

removeExclamationMarks.description: Whether to replace exclamation marks with periods

mode.description: VAD aggressiveness level (0–3). Higher values are more aggressive at filtering non-speech. Default: 2.

frameDurationMs.description: Duration of each VAD processing frame in milliseconds. Must be 10, 20, or 30. Default: 20.

silencePaddingMs.description: Amount of silence (in ms) to prepend before the detected speech start as a pre-roll buffer. Default: 300.

autoEndSilenceDurationMs.description: Duration of silence (in ms) after speech that triggers end-of-utterance detection. Default: 800.

settings.description: ASR-specific settings including model, language preferences, etc.

unintelligiblePlaceholder.description: Placeholder text to use when speech is unintelligible or cannot be transcribed

voiceActivityDetection.description: Whether to enable voice activity detection to automatically start/stop recording based on speech presence

allOf.description: Server-side VAD configuration. When set, the server autonomously detects speech boundaries — clients send continuous audio without calling start/end_user_voice_input.

language.description: The language code for speech recognition (e.g., 'en-US')

dictionaryPhrases.description: The phrases to add to the speech recognition dictionary

audioFormat.description: Audio input format for speech recognition (e.g., "pcm_16000")

modelId.description: Model ID to use for transcription (e.g., "scribe_v2_realtime"), defaults to scribe_v2_realtime

audioFormat.description: Audio encoding format for speech-to-text, defaults to pcm_16000

languageCode.description: Language code in ISO 639-1 or ISO 639-3 format (e.g., "en", "es")

includeTimestamps.description: Whether to receive word-level timestamps in transcription results, defaults to false

includeLanguageDetection.description: Whether to include detected language code in transcription results, defaults to false

commitStrategy.description: Strategy for committing transcriptions - manual or voice activity detection, defaults to manual

vadSilenceThresholdSecs.description: Silence threshold in seconds for VAD (0.3-3), defaults to 1.5

vadThreshold.description: Threshold for voice activity detection (0.1-0.9), defaults to 0.4

minSpeechDurationMs.description: Minimum speech duration in milliseconds (50-2000), defaults to 100

minSilenceDurationMs.description: Minimum silence duration in milliseconds (50-2000), defaults to 100

enableLogging.description: When false, zero retention mode is used (enterprise only), defaults to true

modelId.description: Model ID to use for transcription (e.g., "nova-3", "nova-2", "base", "enhanced"), defaults to nova-3

audioFormat.description: Audio encoding format for speech-to-text, defaults to pcm_16000

interimResults.description: Enable interim (partial) transcription results during streaming, defaults to false

endpointing.description: Milliseconds of silence to wait before finalizing speech (10+) or false to disable, defaults to 300

smartFormat.description: Apply formatting (punctuation, capitalization, currency, etc.) to improve readability, defaults to true

punctuate.description: Add punctuation and capitalization to transcript, defaults to true

diarize.description: Recognize and label different speakers in the audio, defaults to false

utteranceEndMs.description: Milliseconds to wait before sending UtteranceEnd event (use with interim_results)

vadEvents.description: Send SpeechStarted events when speech is detected, defaults to false

sampleRate.description: Audio sample rate in Hz (8000, 16000, 22050, 24000, 44100), defaults to 16000

formatTurns.description: Enable formatted transcripts with capitalization and punctuation (adds latency, not recommended for voice agents), defaults to false

speechModel.description: Speech model to use: English-only or multilingual (supports English, Spanish, French, German, Italian, Portuguese), defaults to universal-streaming-english

keytermsPrompt.description: List of custom words or phrases to improve recognition accuracy

vadThreshold.description: Voice activity detection confidence threshold (0.0 to 1.0) for classifying audio frames as silence, defaults to 0.4

endOfTurnConfidenceThreshold.description: Confidence threshold (0.0 to 1.0) for determining end of turn, defaults to 0.4

minEndOfTurnSilenceWhenConfident.description: Minimum silence in milliseconds required to detect end of turn when confident, defaults to 400

maxTurnSilence.description: Maximum silence in milliseconds allowed in a turn before triggering end of turn, defaults to 1280

inactivityTimeout.description: Time in seconds of inactivity before session is terminated (5-3600), no timeout if not set

audioFormat.description: Audio input format for speech recognition, defaults to pcm_16000

transcriptionMode.description: Transcription mode: "standard" for faster processing or "enhanced" for higher accuracy, defaults to standard

enablePunctuation.description: Enable automatic punctuation in transcripts, defaults to true

enableFormatting.description: Enable automatic formatting (numbers, dates, currency, etc.), defaults to true

additionalVocab.description: Custom vocabulary words or phrases to improve recognition accuracy

enableDiarization.description: Enable speaker diarization to detect different speakers, defaults to false

maxDelay.description: Maximum delay in seconds for transcription results (0-10), lower values reduce latency

enabled.description: Whether content moderation is enabled for this project

llmProviderId.description: ID of the LLM provider used for moderation (must support moderation API, e.g. OpenAI or Mistral)

blockedCategories.description: List of category names that should cause the input to be blocked. If omitted or empty, any flagged category will block the input. Category names are provider-specific. OpenAI categories: harassment, harassment/threatening, hate, hate/threatening, illicit, illicit/violent, self-harm, self-harm/instructions, self-harm/intent, sexual, sexual/minors, violence, violence/graphic. Mistral categories: sexual, hate_and_discrimination, violence_and_threats, dangerous_and_criminal_content, selfharm, health, financial, law, pii.

mode.description: Moderation execution mode. "strict" (default): moderation runs before all other processing — the turn is held until the moderation result is available. "standard": moderation runs after filler generation, in parallel with classification/knowledge retrieval (processTextInput), reducing perceived latency while still blocking flagged input before classification results are acted upon.

defaultClassifierId.description: ID of the classifier used to evaluate sample copy prompt triggers for all stages in this project. Individual sample copies can override this with classifierOverrideId.

llmProviderId.description: ID of the LLM provider used to generate the filler sentence

llmSettings.description: LLM provider-specific settings for filler generation

prompt.description: Prompt instructing the LLM to produce a short neutral filler sentence (e.g. "Generate a single short neutral sentence to fill silence while processing, like "Hmm, let me think about that."")

completion.description: Maximum tokens for completion (response generation) calls

tool.description: Maximum tokens for smart_function tool calls

transformation.description: Maximum tokens for context transformer calls

filler.description: Maximum tokens for filler sentence generation calls

allOf.description: Maximum output token caps per request type. Enforced as a hard ceiling over the entity-level defaultMaxTokens.
Maximum input context token caps per request type. When exceeded, the oldest non-system messages are trimmed from history before the call.

OpenAI categories: harassment, harassment/threatening, hate, hate/threatening, illicit, illicit/violent, self-harm, self-harm/instructions, self-harm/intent, sexual, sexual/minors, violence, violence/graphic. Mistral categories: sexual, hate_and_discrimination, violence_and_threats, dangerous_and_criminal_content, selfharm, health, financial, law, pii.

blockedCategories.description: List of category names that should cause the input to be blocked. If omitted or empty, any flagged category will block the input. Category names are provider-specific. OpenAI categories: harassment, harassment/threatening, hate, hate/threatening, illicit, illicit/violent, self-harm, self-harm/instructions, self-harm/intent, sexual, sexual/minors, violence, violence/graphic. Mistral categories: sexual, hate_and_discrimination, violence_and_threats, dangerous_and_criminal_content, selfharm, health, financial, law, pii.

mode.description: Moderation execution mode. "strict" (default): moderation runs before all other processing — the turn is held until the moderation result is available. "standard": moderation runs after filler generation, in parallel with classification/knowledge retrieval (processTextInput), reducing perceived latency while still blocking flagged input before classification results are acted upon.

get.description: Retrieves audit logs for a specific project

Retrieves a paginated list of audit logs with optional filtering by entity type, action, user, or date range. Use filters for precise queries: entityType, action (CREATE/UPDATE/DELETE), userId, entityId, or date ranges with operators (gte, lte, between).

get.description: Returns the available analytics sources with their queryable dimensions and metrics. Use this to discover what can be queried via the /analytics/query endpoint.

Generic analytics query engine. Specify a source, metrics to aggregate, optional groupBy dimensions, time interval for bucketing, and filters. Use normalizeBy to enable two-phase aggregation: metrics are first summed within each unit of that dimension (e.g. conversationId), then the requested aggregation function is applied across those sums — enabling queries like "average total tokens per conversation". Returns flat rows with dimension values and computed metrics. Use GET /analytics/sources to discover available sources, dimensions, and metrics.

schema.description: Dimension IDs to group results by (max 5)

parameters.description: Dimension IDs to group results by (max 5)
parameters.name: groupBy

schema.description: Time bucket interval for time-series aggregation

parameters.description: Time bucket interval for time-series aggregation
parameters.name: interval

schema.description: Dimension ID to use as the inner aggregation unit for two-phase aggregation. When set, metrics are first summed within each (groupBy + normalizeBy) group, then the requested aggregation function is applied across those sums. Example: normalizeBy=conversationId with avg:promptTokens gives the average total prompt tokens per conversation. Not compatible with the bare "count" metric.

parameters.description: Dimension ID to use as the inner aggregation unit for two-phase aggregation. When set, metrics are first summed within each (groupBy + normalizeBy) group, then the requested aggregation function is applied across those sums. Example: normalizeBy=conversationId with avg:promptTokens gives the average total prompt tokens per conversation. Not compatible with the bare "count" metric.
parameters.name: normalizeBy

parameters.description: Relative time range (e.g. { amount: 7, unit: "days" }). Mutually exclusive with from/to — takes precedence if all three are provided.
parameters.name: relativeTime

parameters.description: End of the date range (inclusive). ISO 8601 format. Ignored when relativeTime is set.
parameters.name: to

parameters.description: Additional equality filters: key = dimension ID, value = exact match value
parameters.name: filters

get.description: Produces a self-contained JSON bundle of all migratable config entities. Intended to be called by a remote instance during a server-side pull. Pass one or more ID arrays (projectIds, stageIds, agentIds, …) to select specific entities — all transitive FK dependencies are resolved automatically so the bundle is always self-consistent. An empty query (no params) exports everything. Provider config (API credentials) is stripped from exported records.

get.summary: Export a project as an exchange bundle
get.description: Produces a self-contained, provider-agnostic exchange bundle for the specified project. All child entities (agents, stages, classifiers, context transformers, tools, global actions, guardrails, knowledge base) are included. Provider UUID references are replaced by provider hints (`type` + `apiType`) so the bundle can be imported into any environment. Credentials are never included. Entity IDs in the bundle are preserved as local cross-references and remapped to fresh UUIDs on import.

post.summary: Import a project from an exchange bundle
post.description: Imports a project from a provider-agnostic exchange bundle. All entity IDs are remapped to fresh UUIDs so repeated imports never overwrite existing data. Provider hints are resolved to local provider IDs by matching `type` + `apiType` (first match wins). If no matching local provider is found for a hint, the corresponding provider field is set to null. Returns the newly assigned project ID and a count of created entities.

post.summary: Exchange SDP offer/answer for WebRTC session
post.description: Accepts a WebRTC SDP offer from the client and returns an SDP answer with all ICE candidates embedded (gather-and-return; no trickle ICE). Before creating the offer the client must open two named DataChannels: "control" (ordered: true) for all JSON messages (same protocol as WebSocket) and "audio" (ordered: false, maxRetransmits: 0) for binary audio frames. Audio frame format: [uint16 LE: turnId byte length] [turnId UTF-8 bytes] [raw PCM audio]. Once the DataChannels are open, authenticate by sending an "auth" JSON message over the control channel.

sdpOffer.description: SDP offer string generated by the client RTCPeerConnection

sdpAnswer.description: SDP answer with all ICE candidates embedded (gather-and-return, no trickle ICE)

500.description: Failed to create peer connection or generate ICE answer
